checks.description: Verify all 33GOD infrastructure services are running and healthy
checks.prompt:
INFRA HEALTH CHECK — Verify all 33GOD services.

1. Run: docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}' | grep 33god
2. Check each service is running and healthy: bloodbank, ws-relay, candystore, holocene, heartbeat-tick, postgres, redis.
3. Check RabbitMQ (auto-detect): RABBIT=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^(33god-rabbitmq|theboard-rabbitmq)$' | head -1); docker exec

rabbitmqctl list_queues name messages consumers
4. If any service is down or unhealthy: attempt restart with docker compose up -d {service}.
5. Report findings to Grolf (agent:eng:main) if any issues found.

checks.description: Monitor Docker resource usage and container health
checks.prompt:
DOCKER STATUS CHECK — Monitor resource usage.

1. Run: docker stats --no-stream --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}'
2. Flag any container using >80% memory or >50% CPU sustained.
3. Check disk usage: df -h /var/lib/docker
4. If disk >80%: run docker system prune --force (only dangling images/containers).
5. Report any concerns to Grolf.

checks.description: Deep RabbitMQ health check — queues, consumers, message rates
checks.prompt:
RABBITMQ HEALTH CHECK — Deep queue inspection.

1. Check queue depths (auto-detect): RABBIT=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^(33god-rabbitmq|theboard-rabbitmq)$' | head -1); docker exec

rabbitmqctl list_queues name messages consumers
2. Flag any queue with >1000 unacked messages (consumer may be stuck).
3. Check exchange bindings: rabbitmqctl list_bindings
4. Verify bloodbank.events.v1 exchange exists with correct type (topic).
5. Check for DLQ buildup (any queue ending in .dlq with messages > 0).
6. Report to Grolf if action needed.
